title: Wild Rift Patch Notes 7.0d
paragraphs: Welcome to Patch 7.0d! In this patch we have some balance adjustments across the map in our regular and AAA ARAM modes, the introduction of Mel, and some new items. Let’s dive in.
Reminder: content will be released throughout the patch.

Mel Medarda is the presumed heir of the Medarda family, once one of the most powerful in Noxus. In appearance she is a graceful aristocrat, but beneath the surface lies a skilled politician who makes it her business to know everything about everyone she meets. After an encounter with the mysterious Black Rose, Mel discovered the depths of her mother's deception and, for once, faced a situation potentially beyond her control. With newly awakened magical abilities, she sailed home in search of answers—and though many still seek to temper the light within her, Mel's soul remains forever defiant.
Mel will be released on March 13 at 00:01 UTC.

Fiddlesticks has been underperforming across the board and several unintended mechanical tricks have made his mastery path unclear. Because of this, the team aims to remove some of the unclear power and shift strength back into his core gameplay. Additionally, his passive felt unclear, leaving foes unsure how to respond, while also lacking meaningful strategic value. So, we’re increasing the payoff and overall damage and crowd control capability, while reducing the reliance on tricks and narrowing the gap between his skill floor and ceiling. We’re also improving the clarity of his passive effigies, making both the counterplay and effects upon triggering more distinct and impactful.
[Removed] Original Effect
[New] After a takedown, Summon a Scarecrow Effigy that walks toward the nearest enemy. After 3s, or upon reaching the target, it explodes, fearing nearby enemy champions.
[Removed] No longer fears targets along the path.
[New] Fears a selected target.
[New] When casting Bountiful Harvest, Fiddlestick is slowed by 30%
[New] If the ability completes its full channel, or if all targets within its range are killed before it ends, 50% of its cooldown is refunded.
[New] After 1s, the ability can be recast to end it early
[New] During the channel, Fiddlesticks cannot use other abilities or basic attacks.
Damage per second: 60/90/120/150 + 25% Ability Power → 60/90/120/150 + 35% Ability Power
Damage equal to the target’s missing Health: 10/11.5/13/14.5% → 12/14/16/18%
Damage per 0.25s: 25/42.5/60 + 10% Ability Power → 30/50/70 + 10% Ability Power

We found that Xayah’s ultimate with auto-aim was unreliable when there were no nearby attackable targets, preventing it from effectively dodging incoming ranged attacks. So, we’ve optimized its usability.
[New] The ability can now be cast by tapping, even when there are no nearby targets.

Rell’s been feeling a little unbridled lately. With rune changes leaving her durability lagging behind other supports, we’re boosting her survivability and early game trading power to help her get back in the saddle.
Base Attack Damage: 58 → 62

Norra is currently overperforming, with her second skill’s crowd control making her too dominant in the support role. We’re reducing its damage and increasing its mid to late game cooldown to give foes more opportunities for counterplay.

Mordekaiser has struggled to feel the impact of the bonus Attack Damage stolen by his ultimate. We’ve added a bonus AD ratio to his first ability, making the stolen stats more meaningful and giving him more item build options.
Damage: 80/110/140/170(+4-70(Base on Level))(+70% Ability Power)) → 80/110/140/170(+4-70(Base on Level))(+70%Ability Power)(+120% bonus Attack Damage）
Hecarim’s been running circles in higher levels of play because of his early roaming power. We’re reining in his early damage and giving him a stronger late game gallop.
Base Attack Damage: 58 → 54
Attack Damage per level: 3.6 → 4
We’re giving marksmen a brand new on-hit attack item. This will provide you with a large amount of damage in sustained combat.
Build Path: Noonquiver (1350) + Long Sword (500) + Dagger (500) + 550
Every third attack deals 120~160 (based on level) (110~150 (based on level) for ranged champions) bonus physical damage, based on the target’s missing Health, up to a maximum of 50% increased damage.

We’re giving supports a new item dedicated to protecting a single teammate, after equipping this item, you can take a portion of damage on behalf of your teammate, while also gaining a healing effect.
Build Path: Kindlegem (1000) + Chain Vest (900) + 700
When you buy the item, you can designate an ally champion who is Worthy. (You can re-designate another champion by selling and repurchasing the item)
When your Worthy ally is nearby, 12% of the damage they take is redirected to you, and you are healed for 10% of the damage they deal to champions.
We’re giving fighters a brand new comeback item, allowing them to gain a large amount of Attack Damage at low Health to complete a counterattack.
Build Path: Jaurim's Fist (1200) + Jaurim's Fist (1200) + 800
Gain Attack Damage equal to 2.5% of your bonus Health.
Gain up to 9% increased Attack Damage based on your missing Health. Reaches the maximum value when below 30% Health.
We’re giving tank players a sustained combat item, allowing them to deal damage to enemies while continuously gaining healing during combat.
Chain Vest (900) + Negatron Cloak (900) + Ruby Crystal (500) + 700

While in combat with champions, every 4 seconds deal magic damage equal to 3% of your maximum Health to nearby enemy champions, and heal yourself for 250% of the damage dealt, Anguish is not affected by item Ability Haste.
We’re giving fighters a brand new ultimate ability item, allowing them to gain a large amount of power after using your ultimate ability.
Build Path: Jaurim's Fist（1200) + Phage（1000）+ Dagger（500）+ 300
Gain 20 Ultimate Ability Haste
After casting the ultimate ability, gain 40% (20% for ranged) Attack Speed and 20% (10% for ranged) Move Speed for 8 seconds. (30 second cooldown)
We have adjusted the way Titanic Hydra gains its stats, it is no longer tied to Health, and will now directly grant a certain amount of Attack Damage upon purchase.
[New] Attack Damage: 40
Gain 15 + 2.5% bonus Health as Attack Damage
In this patch, 15 brand new Augments have been added. Additionally, there are four previously popular Augments and 8 Easter Eggs returning.
Panacea, Gunslinger, Vitality Balance, Recharge, Rock Solid, Swift Sword, Swiftgate, Life From Death, Blade's Call, Charged Blast, Puppetry, Ability Echo, Sword Stack, Soul Snare, One Inch Long.
For the specific effects of the Augments, you can check the Augment Archive after the update.
Supremacy, Battle Arsenal, Moonshadow Staff, Original Ravenous Hydra.
For the specific effects of the Augments, you can check the Augment Archive after the update.
Limit Break, Storage Space, Starting Line, Next-Gen Technology, Card Master, Lunar Refresh, One More Time, Rabble Rousing.
For the specific effects of the Easter Egg, you can check the Augment Archive after the update.
Cleansing Flame Base Health Regeneration: 30~80 → 15~60
Health Regeneration per Burn Source: 20 → 10
Gold gained when taking damage: 10~20 → 15~30
Can now be obtained repeatedly
Gold granted in Rounds 9, 11, 12: 1000 → 2000

Fixed a bug where Zoe’s Sleepy Trouble Bubble with auto-aim would search for the target’s position an extra time. In simple terms, this made Zoe’s Sleepy Trouble Bubble less accurate when auto-aim is active.
Releasing March 06 @ 00:01 UTC
Releasing March 06 @ 00:01 UTC
Releasing March 06 @ 00:01 UTC
Releasing March 13 @ 00:01 UTC
Love Confession Jayce
Releasing March 13 @ 00:01 UTC
